title: CLC/TS 50701 Safety-Standards Coordination — every cyber control change re-evaluated against EN 50126/8/9/EN 50657
exclusions: Excludes changes formally documented as having no safety relevance with senior cyber-safety sign-off. Excludes purely-IT changes outside the SuC. Excludes changes during the formal pre-commissioning phase (those follow new-build acceptance).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.55.18` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `clc_clause="CLC-TS-50701-c10-1"`. Every row pins (change_id, change_description, en_50126_assessment_at, en_50128_assessment_at, en_50129_assessment_at, en_50657_assessment_at, isa_concurrence_at, isa_concurrence_signed_by, coord_status). Dashboard `CLC/TS 50701 — Safety Standards Coordination` shows coordination posture.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 10.1 requires coordination between CLC/TS 50701 cyber controls and the rail safety-standard ecosystem (EN 50126 RAMS, EN 50128 software, EN 50129 systems, EN 50657 rolling-stock software). UC-22.55.18 instruments the coordination evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves every cyber control change is assessed against the applicable rail safety standards.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.55.18` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `CLC/TS 50701 — Safety Standards Coordination` dashboard, and per-change safety assessment.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Implement a cyber control change without `en_50126_assessment_at`. UC-22.55.18 MUST flag `coord_status="en_50126_missing"`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Run EN 50126/8/9/657 assessments per applicability; ISA concurs. UC-22.55.18 MUST record `coord_status="compliant"`.

dataSources: KV Store `rail_safety_coord_lookup`. ServiceNow Change Management. ISA-issued concurrence documents.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup rail_safety_coord_lookup
| where change_implemented_at IS NOT NULL
| eval coord_status = case(
    en_50126_applicable=true AND isnull(en_50126_assessment_at), "en_50126_missing",
    en_50128_applicable=true AND isnull(en_50128_assessment_at), "en_50128_missing",
    en_50129_applicable=true AND isnull(en_50129_assessment_at), "en_50129_missing",
    en_50657_applicable=true AND isnull(en_50657_assessment_at), "en_50657_missing",
    isa_required=true AND isnull(isa_concurrence_at), "isa_concurrence_missing",
    1=1, "compliant")
| where coord_status != "compliant"
| table change_id change_description en_50126_assessment_at en_50128_assessment_at en_50129_assessment_at en_50657_assessment_at isa_concurrence_at coord_status
description: Verifies cyber control changes are coordinated with EN 50126/8/9 and EN 50657 safety standards.
value: Without safety-standards coordination, cyber changes can compromise rail safety certifications. This UC enforces the linkage.
implementation: (1) Maintain `rail_safety_coord_lookup`. (2) Head of Safety + ISA reviewers sign per applicable standard. (3) Schedule UC-22.55.18 weekly Monday 07:00.
visualization: (Panel 1) Coordination posture per change. (Panel 2) Missing-assessment queue. (Panel 3) ISA-pending queue.
knownFalsePositives:
1. **Phased change** — multi-phase change with phased safety assessments. Suppression: track per-phase status.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `clc_22_55_18_exceptions` with documented Head-of-Safety approval and 30-day maximum grant.
reviewer: Rail Cybersecurity SME Panel + Head of Safety + ISA
equipmentModels: Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, audit_evidence summary index)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- KV Store `rail_safety_coord_lookup`.
- Head of Safety and ISA roles defined.

## Step 1 — Coordination collection

```ini
[rail_safety_coord_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.change_id = string
field.change_description = string
field.change_implemented_at = number
field.en_50126_applicable = bool
field.en_50128_applicable = bool
field.en_50129_applicable = bool
field.en_50657_applicable = bool
field.en_50126_assessment_at = number
field.en_50128_assessment_at = number
field.en_50129_assessment_at = number
field.en_50657_assessment_at = number
field.isa_required = bool
field.isa_concurrence_at = number
field.isa_concurrence_signed_by = string
```

## Step 2 — Search and alert

```ini
[UC-22.55.18: CLC/TS 50701 Safety Standards Coordination]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * 1
dispatch.earliest_time = -1y@y
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.55.18,reg=CLC-TS-50701,clause=CLC-TS-50701-c10-1
action.notable.param.rule_title = Rail safety-standards coordination gap
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 — Operationalize

**Runbook for any `*_missing`** (owner: Head of Safety, severity P2): complete assessment; sign; record.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

**CLC/TS 50701-specific failure modes**:

- **EN 50128 software-safety reviews** can take weeks; track schedule.
- **ISA independence**: ISA must be independent of engineering and operations.
grandmaExplanation: Every cyber-security change to our railway must also be checked by safety experts against railway safety rulebooks (EN 50126/8/9/657). We track each rule-check; if any is missing, the team is alerted.
